sections.paragraph:
fut. יִיעַד-

(1) to point out, to define, to appoint, especially a place, Jer 47:7, and time, 2Sa 20:5 also, a punishment, Mic 6:9.

(2) to espouse, a wife or concubine, Exo 21:8, Exo 21:9. (Arab. وعد to point out anything before, especially good; but also sometimes, to threaten some harm. III. to appoint a time or place.-Apparently cognate is הֹודִיעַ to cause to know, to point out.)

Niphal נֹועַד-

(1) to meet with any one at an appointed place, to come with him to such a place; followed by לְ Exo 25:22 29:42 , 43 Exo 29:43 30:6 , 36 Exo 30:36followed by אֶל Num 10:4.

(2) recipr. to come together at an appointed time or place, Neh 6:2, 10 Job 2:11 Amo 3:3 also generally, to come together, Jos 11:5 1Ki 8:5 followed by עַל against any one, speaking of confederates, Num 14:35 16:11 27:3 .

Hiphil הֹועִיד to appoint any one to meet at a certain time or place, specially for judgment; to summon to a court, to call on to plead. Job 9:19, מִי יֹועִידֵנִי “who shall cite me?” Jer 49:19 50:44 .

Hophal-

(1) to be appointed, set, Jer 24:1.

(2) to be turned, as the face, Eze 21:21.

Derivatives, עֵדָה, מֹועֵד, מֹועָד, מוּעָדָה, and pr.n. נֹועַדְיָה.
